title: yabea microcarpa
intro: Yabea microcarpa, also known as False Flax, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Yabea microcarpa has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared for herbal use by boiling the leaves or roots, and in some cases, the seeds are used to make a medicinal oil or tea.
